steps.title: Spot what behold describes
steps.content: The key signal is that “behold” targets a visual object — “steep and lofty cliffs” — within a context of re-encountering a landscape. The verb describes an act of perception.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The underlined word behold needs to mean the speaker's act of perceiving the cliffs again, since the poem opens "Once again / Do I [blank] these steep and lofty cliffs." Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Regard: to look at with attention or consider — fits the act of visually observing the cliffs upon returning to the landscape. ✔
  • Choice B — Possess: to own or have something — the speaker is looking at the cliffs, not claiming ownership of them; possession is a physical relationship, not a perceptual one. ✘
  • Choice C — Escape: to get away from something — the opposite of encountering; the speaker is returning to and perceiving the cliffs, not fleeing them. ✘
  • Choice D — Neglect: to pay no attention to — directly contradicts the act of deliberately looking at the cliffs; the speaker is focused on them. ✘
That confirms A is correct.
